Detective Delaqua,  I must thank you for delving into the depths of the latest underground crime ring to operate out of Divinity's Reach. I'm sure the people of this fine city would thank you for your tireless efforts at making it a safer place to dwell. More importantly, Miss Iaera's family will be relieved to have their daughter's body returned to them and not rotting away in some crypt beneath the city. That poor girl's death should be mourned. I can only imagine what she endured in those final days.  As always, thank you for trusting a faceless benefactor. I will write again, should any more intel arrive at my door.  —E
Detective Rama (Return to End of Dragons)
Detective's Scribbled Notes (Picking Up the Pieces)

Ding-a-ling! The Mad King was seen busting out of the lion statue in Lion's Arch! The statue lies in ruin, and there's a mad portal in its place. Enter at your own risk. There's no longer any doubt that the Mad King really does exist, and the Durmand Priory believes he's trying to break out into Tyria. Their projections for what will happen if he succeeds are apocalyptic. Word is spreading quickly that, in his current state of power and rage, the Mad King must not be allowed to leave his realm!—Captain Tokk
diplomacy and courtesy, even with enemies.

Dire days. Damned days...    The gilded who've traveled through the veil have built an immunity to the gate's poison, but the gildless are vulnerable. They're falling ill by the day... By the hour... They've been maddened! Our children!    Otho has left the city now—I suspect to protect his kin—leaving myself and Manikaz to our work. And the Arch Steward is silent. I admittedly have little urge to continue, myself, and the Blood Phenom never leaves his laboratory. I can smell it from the library.    Was bringing the gate here an act of desperation? Was there ever hope for us, and is prophecy unpreventable?
